sections.paragraph: an unused root, which had, I suppose, the same meaning as Eth. በቈለ፡ baquala, and በቊለ፡ baqueûla, to germinate, to sprout (ב and מ being interchanged); whence በቊል፡ sprout, scion, twig; whence there is the secondary verb ተበቀለ፡ tabakkala, to punish; prop. as it appears to me, to strike with a rod, although these roots are altogether separated by Ludolf, in Lex. page 238. We must, however, avoid comparing Latin baculus, which is from the stock βάω, pr. Gehftock; compare βακτήριον. Hence-
